notes: Auto-executed tool call, gate-less happy path for Gemini CLI. The recipe drives ONE auto-approved shell tool call ('echo hello') under `gemini -y` (yolo), then a terminal 'done' reply. Canonical arc: ready→working→ready, state NEVER enters waiting. DAEMON STORY (grounded in testdata/real-toolcall-session.jsonl + parser.go): the opening user prompt → user_message drives ready→working; the assistant 'gemini' message carrying toolCalls → ToolUses keeps the session working (content tool-calling, not terminal); the user-role functionResponse message → function_call_output closes the open tool WITHOUT settling; the final 'gemini' message with non-empty content and zero toolCalls → turn_done settles working→ready. Under yolo every tool auto-approves so PermissionPending is never set and waiting is unreachable. SESSION IDENTITY: Gemini stores its transcript as a *.jsonl rollout under ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/ (FilesUnderRoot, keyed by sessionId/UUID) — like claudecode/codex, NOT aider's proc-<PID>. The launcher PID binds via cwd harvested from the bootstrap <session_context> (the heap-bump V8 re-exec is excluded by isHeapBumpWorker). To avoid a transient proc-<PID> presession stealing identity, session_birth anchors to recording start UNPINNED; turn_start establishes the bindable session and turn_end/teardown chain off it. INTERACTIVE recipe (not headless `gemini -p`): Gemini emits no explicit end-of-turn marker, so the parser synthesizes working→ready from a terminal text turn; keeping the REPL alive across send + wait_turn plus an ≥8s trailing sleep lets the daemon observe the full settle before process_exited, then one tmux kill yields one clean process_exited. PROVISIONAL: the turn-end edge is emission-heuristic (parser.go:209) — this expected.jsonl is the documented target the live recording validates and promotes from provisional to settled. The phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the auto-executed tool call is asserted via the single uninterrupted working span plus the no-waiting invariant, not a dedicated phase; tool visibility is coarse (call id+name + functionResponse close). Observations assert the websocket metric vector: cost and tokens non-zero (Gemini bills per-message input/output/cached) and agent gemini-cli; model is left unpinned because the live default is account-dependent (fixture shows gemini-3-flash-preview).

text: Session appears in ready shortly after gemini -y launches
Launcher PID bound via cwd from the bootstrap <session_context> (heap-bump worker excluded)
User prompt requiring a tool call → working (the auto-executed run_shell_command runs inside this span)
The shell tool auto-executes under yolo (no approval gate) and the terminal text turn completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
REPL torn down once after the turn → a single process_exited on the session; state immediately before teardown is ready
